close_reason: Positive observation: atomic temp-then-rename is correct
description: lib.rs:145-184 implements the atomic-write pattern correctly: tempfile in same parent dir (cross-device-rename impossible), create_new(true) so a stale tmp_path cannot be silently overwritten with inherited permissions, mode(0o600) at create time so the kernel applies it atomically with O_CREAT (and 0o600 & ~umask = 0o600 for any reasonable umask), AtomicU64 counter + std::process::id() in tmp_name to avoid same-process and cross-process collisions, error-path cleanup of tempfile. A future contributor might 'simplify' this to fs::write or skip create_new — both regressions. Comments at lines 134-144 and 162-164 already document why; preserve them.
title: what is good: atomic temp-then-rename with create_new(true) + mode(0o600) is correctly defended
